section.heading: Severance Pay
section.text: The purpose of the severance pay program is to provide equitable compensation to certain executive-level employees (Agency Director, Deputy Director, or Bureau Head) who are not in the Civil Service ("at-will"), upon their separation from city service for a qualifying reason.

para.name: scope
para.heading: SCOPE
para.text: This policy applies to all non-civil service employees who fall under the direction and authority of the Mayor of the City of Baltimore in the capacity of a Bureau Head or above who are separating from employment for any reason that is not nonfeasance, misfeasance, or malfeasance. Severance pay is not related to employee leave payouts as described in AM-205-7 "Separation and Payment at Termination".

para.name: definitions
para.heading: DEFINITIONS
para.text: Base pay. "Base pay" means an employee's salary, which may be reported as an hourly wage, weekly, semimonthly, monthly or annually, excluding shift differentials, temporary pay, benefits, overtime, or other similar non-base pay compensation.
Malfeasance. "Malfeasance" means an occurrence when an act that caused an employee separation is intentional.
Misfeasance. "Misfeasance" means an occurrence when an act that cause an employee separation is accidental.
Nonfeasance. "Nonfeasance" means an occurrence when an employee separates for failure to act when action is required.

Severance pay shall be conditioned upon salaries saved by not filling the position for the period of time paid in severance and will only be paid when funds are available in the agency's budget. Severance pay is calculated on base pay only.

tr.td: At least one (1) year but less than three (3) years
At least three (3) years but less than six (6) years
At least six (6) years but less than ten (10) years

para.text: Severance pay is contingent upon the non-civil service employee signing the severance agreement and waiving all claims against the City relating to termination of employment with the exception of Unemployment and Worker's Compensations laws.
Severance pay does not include any payment which an employee is entitled to for any other benefits accrued and vested to the employee.
This severance policy does not and shall not create employment or compensation rights.
